lines.line: Bring one spear-optimised PC and open every battle with a General Duel (scale_transitions §3.7): my PC cannot die (ED-898, worst=recoverable capture) while the enemy NPC general I wound goes Stage1→Stage2→Command 0, dropping their whole army to the 1-die/Line/no-tactics floor. 'The general IS the battle' — I've won before formation, composition, thread or discipline ever mattered.
lines.permitting_rules: scale_transitions §3.7 (Priority-8 duel every turn); mass_battle §A.1 axiom 'the general is the battle'; §A.5 ED-898 two-stage incap→Command 0 + 'no mechanic kills a PC'; §A.6/PP-273 uncommanded 1-die floor
lines.what_breaks: Omega non-dominance (solvable strategy) + the entire Part A choice tree (formations §A.6, tactics §A.8, thread §A.10, composition §A.4) becomes optional flavour

lines.line: Always split across all simultaneous engagements — canon itself says split beats concentration by up to 45%, and the only counter is holding a Narrow Pass. Concentration is a trap option.
lines.permitting_rules: mass_battle §A.8 PP-508/ED-358: 'Split dominates concentration by +9% to +45% win-rate ... suboptimal ONLY when Narrow Pass or Size=1'
lines.what_breaks: Omega non-dominance, admitted in-doc; the concentrate-vs-split tactical axis (§A.8 Concentration/Fibonacci) collapses to one answer

lines.line: Fight Company-scale engagements not War-scale, so Thread ops cost 0 Coherence/op instead of −2 (and the −1/op cap text says War can't cost −2 anyway) — the same force-split that already wins militarily also zeroes my substrate cost.
lines.permitting_rules: mass_battle §A.10 scale table (Company 0/op, War −2/op) contradicted by the −1/op cap note + PP-501 1/turn example; scale set by force size §A.3; compounds PP-508
lines.what_breaks: Omega non-dominance + cross-scale consequence; the P-14 'risk the substrate?' thread juncture is void at small scale and self-contradicting at War

lines.line: Defender sits in Shield Wall behind Walls forever: +3 DR + +2D Def on every pool, no flanking possible, and the idle-army Morale penalty is explicitly waived for me — cost-free turtle whose only threat is Artillery my besieger bleeds Treasury to field.
lines.permitting_rules: §A.9 Walls (+3 DR, no flanking, Slow cannot advance); §A.6/PP-500 Shield Wall +2D Def blanket; §A.7 Phase-7 idle-clock terrain exception; §A.14b attacker Campaign Supply −100/season
lines.what_breaks: besieged-defender decision tree collapses to a single no-downside 'hold'; N-grounded (siege realism) so flag-not-reject

lines.line: Before every battle I clear a soft social + investigation + combat scene to stack +1D Command, +1D first Volley, and a free Reform on turn 1 — free, uncapped in count, every single time.
lines.permitting_rules: scale_transitions §3.8 (PP-261/ED-151): three distinct bonus channels, no cost, no feeder-scene cap, 'most relevant unit; 1 turn duration'
lines.what_breaks: mild non-dominance: scene-vs-battle tempo degenerates to a mandatory pre-battle farming ritual; bounded by +1D/1-turn so low magnitude

findings.claim: A combat-optimised PC can risk-free-decapitate any enemy army via repeated General Duels — ED-898's PC no-death guarantee removes all downside while incapacitating the enemy general zeroes their Command and floors their whole army, so 'the general is the battle' becomes a dominant mass-layer bypass that flattens all of Part A.

Canon itself states splitting dominates concentration by +9–45% win-rate with the sole exceptions of Narrow Pass terrain or the Size=1 edge, so the headline concentrate-vs-split tactical axis is a solved (non-dominant-violating) choice — resolved in ED-358 as 'intended' without ever applying the Omega non-dominance lens.

The §A.10 War-scale '−2/op' Coherence cost contradicts the adjacent '−1 per operation cap, no additional surcharge' note and PP-501's 1/turn worked example; simultaneously, keying substrate cost to battle scale lets a player split into Company-scale engagements for 0-cost thread ops, compounding PP-508 split-dominance into a double-dominant line.
findings.cite: mass_battle_v30.md §A.10 scale table + cap note + PP-501; §A.3 scale-by-force-size

findings.claim: Walls (+3 DR, no flanking) × Shield Wall (+2D Def blanket) × the Phase-7 idle-Morale terrain waiver produces a cost-free defensive turtle whose besieged decision tree collapses to a single strictly-optimal 'hold' with no morale downside — N-grounded as siege realism, but the idle-waiver removes the 'sortie or rot' tension that would keep it a real choice.
findings.cite: mass_battle_v30.md §A.9 Walls, §A.6/PP-500 Shield Wall, §A.7 Phase-7 idle exception; §A.14b Campaign Supply

findings.claim: §3.8 grants three uncapped, cost-free, distinct pre-battle bonus channels (Social→+1D Command, Investigation→+1D Volley, Combat→free Reform) with no per-battle feeder-scene cap, degenerating the scene-vs-battle tempo choice into a mandatory turn-1 farming ritual any optimal player performs — bounded but non-dominant-eroding.
